title: Mirabilis cedrosensis
taxonomy: Mirabilis cedrosensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. This species is a member of the genus Mirabilis, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as four o'clocks. The family Nyctaginaceae, also known as the evening primrose family, is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas and are characterized by their four- or five-merous flowers. Mirabilis cedrosensis is endemic to the Channel Islands off the coast of California, where it grows in coastal scrub and grasslands. The classification of Mirabilis cedrosensis reflects its unique characteristics and its relationships with other plants in the family Nyctaginaceae.

intro: Mirabilis cedrosensis, also known as the San Clemente Island five-spot, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant endemic to San Clemente Island in California.
